forecasts.reasoning: To resolve NO, the exact word "tariff" or "tariffs" must appear on at least one of the two specified URLs (the NYT Today's Paper index or the WSJ Front Page) every single day for 42 consecutive days (May 20 to June 30, 2025). While Trump's tariff policies are a major ongoing news story, a 42-day uninterrupted streak is extremely difficult to maintain in the fast-paced news cycle. The specified WSJ URL strictly captures the front page, while the NYT URL captures the headlines and summaries of the daily print edition. Crucially, on weekends—such as Sundays when the WSJ typically has no print edition, and Saturdays when the NYT print edition is much thinner with fewer business articles—the likelihood of an omission spikes significantly. The natural volatility of the news cycle guarantees that at least one day during this six-week window will be dominated by other major events (politics, foreign affairs, natural disasters, etc.), leaving "tariff" off the specified pages. Therefore, the probability of the word "disappearing" on at least one day in the timeframe is exceptionally high.
